---
title: "Layout & Formatting"
sidebar: example-sidebar
---

Quarto supports rich layout without writing any HTML or CSS.

## Callouts

::: {.callout-note}
## Note
Use **notes** for background information or definitions.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Tip
Use **tips** for best practices or practical advice.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Warning
Use **warnings** to flag common mistakes.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Important
Use **important** for things students must not miss.
:::

## Columns

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Concept**

The sine function maps an angle to the
y-coordinate on the unit circle.

$$f: \mathbb{R} \to [-1, 1]$$
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Key values**

| $x$ | $0$ | $\frac{\pi}{6}$ | $\frac{\pi}{2}$ | $\pi$ |
|-----|-----|--------|--------|-------|
| $\sin(x)$ | $0$ | $0.5$ | $1$ | $0$ |
:::
::::

## Tabs

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Definition
The sine function is one of the fundamental trigonometric functions.
It is periodic with period $2\pi$ and range $[-1, 1]$.

## Formula
Using the Taylor series expansion:

$$\sin(x) = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{(-1)^n}{(2n+1)!}\, x^{2n+1}
           = x - \frac{x^3}{6} + \frac{x^5}{120} - \cdots$$

## Properties
- **Period:** $2\pi$
- **Range:** $[-1,\, 1]$
- **Zeros:** $x = k\pi,\; k \in \mathbb{Z}$
- **Maxima:** $x = \frac{\pi}{2} + 2k\pi$
:::
